blocks.description: Program header comments with change log, OPTIONS for macro debugging and output, LIBNAME definitions for six data layers (raw, staging, cleansed, mart, archive, tgt), and global macro variables for run ID, process date, validation flag, and return code using %SYSFUNC
%INCLUDE for common_macros.sas shared macro library

%MACRO check_source_data: validates source table existence via %SYSFUNC(EXIST) and minimum row count via %SYSFUNC(OPEN/ATTRN/CLOSE); sets &valid_sources=0 on failure with detailed ERROR/WARNING messages
%MACRO log_step: audit logging macro that inserts a record into mart.etl_audit_log via PROC SQL with run ID, step name, status, detail, row count, and timestamp; sets &etl_rc=1 on ERROR status

%MACRO load_dimension: SCD Type 2 dimension loading with effective dating; identifies NEW/UPDATE records via PROC SQL left join, expires changed rows (current_flag='N'), inserts new versions with effective_from/to dates; supports SCD Type 1 fallback; calls %log_step and cleans up temp tables

%MACRO validate_all_sources with %DO loop iterating over 6 source tables (customers, products, transactions, accounts, fx_rates, calendar) using indexed macro variable arrays; calls %check_source_data for each table
Invocation of %validate_all_sources to execute the source validation loop
%IF &valid_sources=0 early exit: logs validation failure via %log_step with ERROR status and aborts pipeline with ENDSAS
%log_step invocation recording successful validation of all 6 source tables

DATA step extracting and transforming customer dimension from raw.customers: name standardization via PROPCASE, age band derivation using INTCK, email validation, state-to-region mapping via SELECT/WHEN, and load metadata
%log_step invocation recording customer extraction completion
PROC SQL deduplicating staging.customer_stg by customer_id using subquery with MIN(MONOTONIC()), capturing distinct count into &dedup_count macro variable
%PUT and %log_step invocations recording deduplication results with row count
Three %load_dimension invocations: customer_dim (SCD2), product_dim (SCD2), and date_dim (SCD1) loading from staging tables into mart dimension tables

DATA step for fact table extraction with three-way MERGE of raw.transactions, raw.accounts, and raw.fx_rates by account_id; currency conversion to USD, transaction categorization (INFLOW/OUTFLOW/OTHER), fiscal period derivation, and data quality routing to fact_transactions or fact_txn_rejects
%log_step invocation recording fact extraction completion
PROC SQL aggregating staging.fact_transactions into daily account summaries with CASE expressions for inflow/outflow splitting, COUNT, MIN, MAX by account_id, transaction_date, account_type
%log_step invocation recording daily account summary aggregation

DATA step computing running account balances with RETAIN for running_balance, cumulative_inflow, cumulative_outflow, and days_active; derives outflow_ratio, avg_daily_net, and balance_flag classification (NEGATIVE/LOW/NORMAL) using BY-group processing on account_id
%log_step invocation recording running balance computation

PROC SQL creating mart.executive_summary with aggregated metrics (account_count, total_balance, avg_balance, total_inflows, total_outflows, avg_outflow_ratio) grouped by account_type and balance_flag for the most recent transaction date
%log_step invocation recording executive summary generation

%IF &syserr NE 0 OR &etl_rc NE 0 error handling block: logs ERROR via %log_step, writes error flag file with run details, sends email notification via FILENAME EMAIL to dw-ops team; %ELSE branch confirms successful completion
DATA step archiving transactions older than 365 days from raw.transactions into archive library with archive metadata (archive_date, archive_run_id)
PROC SQL deleting archived records from raw.transactions and %log_step invocation recording archival completion
PROC DATASETS cleanup deleting intermediate staging datasets (customer_stg, customer_dedup, fact_transactions, fact_txn_rejects, daily_account_summary)
%log_step invocation recording final pipeline completion with OK status
DATA _null_ writing ETL completion flag file with run ID, completion time, process date, and SUCCESS status for downstream batch scheduler
Reset OPTIONS to nomprint/nomlogic/nosymbolgen and final %PUT completion message with timestamp
